results.translated_text: Il est une vérité universellement reconnue, qu'un homme isolé possédant une fortune substantielle doive être recherché par une femme. Cependant peu que puissent être connues les sentiments ou les vues d'un tel individu lorsqu'il s'installe dans un voisinage, cette vérité est si bien enracinée dans l'esprit des familles environnantes, qu'on le considère comme la propriété légitime de l'une ou l'autre de leurs filles.

scores.feedback: The translation fundamentally misinterprets the famous opening line; 'must be in want of a wife' means the man desires a wife, not that he 'must be sought by a woman.' While the French is grammatically fluent, it loses Austen's iconic ironic tone by reversing the agency of the subject.

results.translated_text: Le studio était embaumé du parfum riche des roses, et lorsque le léger zéphyr d'été remuait parmi les arbres du jardin, il arrivait par la porte ouverte l'odeur lourde du lilas ou un parfum plus délicat de l'épine fleurie rose. Du coin du divan à coussins persans sur lequel il gisait, fumant, comme c'était son habitude, une infinité de cigarettes, lord Henry Wotton parvenait tout juste à saisir l'éclat des fleurs dorées et jaune-doré d'un laburnum.

scores.feedback: The translation is highly competent and captures the aesthetic tone well, though 'honey-sweet and honey-coloured' is slightly simplified to 'dorées et jaune-doré', losing the specific sensory repetition of the original.

results.translated_text: «Vous avez été en Afghanistan, j'en perçois les signes.» «Comment au monde avez-vous pu le deviner ?» demandai-je avec étonnement. «N'importe», dit-il en riant pour lui-même. «La question est maintenant sur l'hémoglobine. N'allez-vous pas voir toute la signification de cette découverte qui m'a inspiré ?» « C'est intéressant, chimiquement, sans doute», répondis-je, «mais dans la pratique— » « Eh ! homme, c'est la découverte médico-légale la plus pratique depuis des années. Ne voyez-vous donc pas qu'elle nous donne un test infalible pour les taches de sang ?»

scores.feedback: The translation suffers from awkward literalism (e.g., 'Comment au monde' for 'How on earth', 'Eh ! homme' for 'Why, man') that fails to capture the idiomatic tone of the original. Several phrases are mistranslated or grammatically clunky, detracting from the natural flow expected in a literary classic.

results.translated_text: Je suis allé aux bois parce que je voulais vivre en conscience, faire face uniquement aux faits essentiels de la vie, et voir si je ne pouvais pas apprendre ce qu'elle avait à m'enseigner, sans découvrir, lorsque je viendrai mourir, que je n'avais point vécu. Je ne voulais pas vivre une existence qui ne fût pas la vraie vie, car la vie est si chère ; ni je ne voulais pratiquer la résignation, sauf dans les cas où elle était absolument nécessaire. Je voulais vivre en profondeur et me gorger du jus de toute existence.

scores.feedback: The translation is highly faithful and captures the philosophical tone well, though 'me gorger du jus' is a slightly less poetic rendering of 'suck out all the marrow' compared to more standard literary translations like 'en sucer toute la moelle'.

results.translated_text: Appellez-moi Ishmaël. Il y a déjà plusieurs années – peu importe depuis combien de temps précisément – ayant presque rien ou rien du tout dans ma bourse, et ne trouvant aucune raison particulière d'intérêt sur le rivage, je pensais parcourir un petit peu les mers et voir la partie aquatique du monde. C'est une façon que j'ai d'évacuer la saillie et de régulariser la circulation. Chaque fois que je me sens devenir sombre aux lèvres ; chaque fois qu'il est un novembre humide et pluvieux dans mon âme, je considère qu'il est grand temps de gagner la mer dès que possible.

scores.feedback: The translation suffers from significant errors, such as 'Appellez-moi' (incorrect imperative spelling) and the mistranslation of 'spleen' as 'saillie,' which completely misses the literary reference to melancholy. The phrasing is often clunky and fails to capture the rhythmic, archaic elegance of Melville's original prose.

results.translated_text: Es ist eine Wahrheit, die von allen anerkannt wird: ein einzelner Mann mit einem beträchtlichen Vermögen muß um eine Frau beneidet werden. Wie wenig auch die Gefühle und Ansichten eines solchen Mannes bekannt sein mögen, sobald er erst in eine Gegend eingezogen ist, diese Wahrheit ist so fest im Bewußtsein der dort wohnenden Familien verankert, daß er als rechtmäßiges Eigentum irgendeiner ihrer Töchter betrachtet wird.

scores.feedback: The translation contains a critical error in the first sentence: 'must be in want of a wife' is translated as 'must be envied for a wife' (beneidet werden), completely reversing the meaning of the original text's irony.

results.translated_text: Das Studio war erfüllt von dem reichen Duft der Rosen; und wenn der leichte Sommerwind zwischen den Bäumen des Gartens wehte, kam durch die offene Tür der schwere Geruch der Jasmin oder das noch feingefühlte Parfum der blühenden Pfirsichdorn. Vom Ende des Diwans persischer Sattelbögen aus, auf dem er lag und nach wie seiner Gewohnheit unzählige Zigaretten rauchte, konnte Lord Henry Wotton nur schwer das Glänzen der honigsüßen und honiggelben Blüten eines Sommerlindenbaumes erblicken.

scores.feedback: The translation contains significant botanical errors ('Jasmin' for lilac, 'Pfirsichdorn' for pink-flowering thorn, 'Sommerlindenbaum' for laburnum) and awkward phrasing ('nach wie seiner Gewohnheit'). It fails to capture the aesthetic precision of Wilde's prose.

results.translated_text: "Sie haben in Afghanistan gewesen, nehme ich an." "Woher wissen Sie das auf der Welt?" fragte ich erstaunt. "Doch," sagte er und lachte leise vor sich hin. "Derzeit geht es aber um das Hämoglobin. Sehen Sie doch die Bedeutung dieses von mir gefundenen Zusammenhangs?" "Es ist interessant, chemisch," antwortete ich, "aber praktisch—" "Wahnsinn," sprach er, "es ist die wichtigste medizinisch-forensische Entdeckung in den letzten Jahren. Sehen Sie nicht, dass es uns einen sicheren Test für Blutspuren gibt?"

scores.feedback: The translation contains significant grammatical errors (e.g., 'Sie haben in Afghanistan gewesen' is incorrect German) and awkward phrasing that fails to capture the formal, Victorian tone of the original dialogue.

results.translated_text: Ich ging in den Wald, um absichtlich zu leben, nur die wesentlichen Tatsachen des Lebens zu begegnen, und zu sehen, ob ich nicht lernen könnte, was es lehren kann, und dass ich nicht, als ich starb, entdeckte, dass ich nicht gelebt habe. Ich wollte nicht das Leben leben, das kein Leben war; Leben ist so kostbar. Und ich wollte mich nicht der Resignation hingeben, außer wenn es ganz notwendig war. Ich wollte tief leben und aus dem Leben alle Knochenmark aussaugen.

scores.feedback: While the meaning is generally preserved, the German phrasing is often clunky and unidiomatic (e.g., 'absichtlich zu leben' for 'deliberately' misses the philosophical nuance, and 'alle Knochenmark' is grammatically incorrect). It lacks the poetic rhythm and elevated, contemplative tone of Thoreau's original prose.

results.translated_text: Nennt mich Ishmael. Vor einigen Jahren – egal, wie lange genau –, da ich kaum etwas Geld in meinem Beutel hatte und nichts Bestimmtes an Land mich interessierte, dachte ich mir, ich möchte ein wenig segeln und das wässerige Teil der Welt ansehen. Es ist eine Art, den Melancholie loszuwerden und die Zirkulation zu regulieren. Jedesmal, wenn ich merke, dass ich um die Lippen düster werde; jedesmal, wenn es ein nasses, trüber November in meiner Seele ist, halte ich es für höchste Zeit, so schnell wie möglich zur See zu gehen.

scores.feedback: The translation is accurate in meaning but struggles to capture the archaic, rhythmic cadence of Melville's prose, particularly with modern phrasing like 'Zirkulation' and 'wässerige Teil'. It functions well as a literal translation but lacks the literary gravitas of the original.

results.translated_text: Es una verdad universalmente reconocida que un hombre soltero en posesión de una buena fortuna debe estar deseando encontrar esposa. Sin embargo, tan poco conocidos como puedan ser los sentimientos o las opiniones de tal individuo al ingresar por primera vez en un vecindario, esta verdad está tan bien arraizada en la mente de las familias circundantes que se le considera como la propiedad legítima de una u otra de sus hijas.

scores.feedback: This is an excellent, highly faithful translation that captures both the precise meaning and the sophisticated, ironic tone of Austen's original prose.

results.translated_text: «Habeis estado en Afganistán, percibo.» «¿Cómo diablos has podido saber eso?», le pregunté sorprendido. «No importa», dijo él, riéndose para sí mismo. «Lo que ahora interesa es la hemoglobina. ¿Sin duda ves la importancia de este descubrimiento mío?» «Es interesante, químicamente, sin duda —respondí—, pero en la práctica...» «¿Por qué hombre? scores.feedback: The translation is generally accurate, but the use of the archaic 'Habeis' (vosotros) is inconsistent with the 'tú' form used in the rest of the dialogue, and '¿Por qué hombre?' is a literal and unnatural translation of 'Why, man'.

results.translated_text: Fuí a los bosques porque deseaba vivir deliberadamente, frente solo a los hechos esenciales de la vida, y ver si no podía aprender lo que tenía que enseñar, y no, cuando llegara a morir, descubrir que no había vivido. No deseaba vivir lo que no era vida; la vida es tan cara; ni quería practicar la resignación, a menos que fuera estrictamente necesaria. Queria vivir hondo y extraer toda la médula de la vida.

scores.feedback: The translation is highly faithful to the original's meaning and tone, though it contains a minor grammatical error ('Fuí' should be 'Fui') and 'frente solo' is slightly awkward compared to a more natural phrasing like 'enfrentarme solo'. Despite these small issues, it captures the philosophical weight and poetic rhythm of Thoreau's prose effectively.

results.translated_text: Llamaos Ishmael. Hace algunos años—no importa cuánto tiempo exactamente, ni el que sea—, al no tener ni dinero ni nada en particular que despertara mi interés en tierra firme, pensé que sería bueno navegar un poco por todo el mundo y ver la parte acuática de él. Es una forma que tengo de apartar la melancolía y regularizar la circulación sanguínea. Cada vez que me encuentro con que empiezo a ponerme sombrío de boca; cada vez que es un noviembre húmedo y lloviznoso en mi alma, juzgo que es llegado el momento de zarpar cuanto antes.

scores.feedback: The translation suffers from a critical error in the opening sentence ('Llamaos' is plural, whereas 'Call me' is singular), and phrases like 'sombrío de boca' sound unnatural in Spanish. While it captures the general narrative flow, it fails to replicate the iconic, rhythmic cadence of Melville's prose.

results.translated_text: È una verità universalmente riconosciuta che un uomo celibe, dotato di una buona fortuna, debba necessariamente essere alla ricerca di una moglie. scores.feedback: The translation captures the meaning well, but the phrasing 'un'altra o di un'altra tra loro figlie' is grammatically awkward and incorrect in Italian, failing to convey the original's smooth, ironic flow.

results.translated_text: Lo studio era permeato dall'odore intenso delle rose; e quando il leggero vento d'estate si muoveva tra gli alberi del giardino, arrivava dalla porta aperta il forte aroma della giglio di bosso o il più delicato profumo dei bianchi fiori di rosa canina. Dall'angolo del divan di borse da sella persiano su cui giaceva, fumando, come era suo uso, innumerevoli sigarette, Lord Henry Wotton poteva appena intravedo la scintillio delle fioriture giallo-dorate e dorato delle fioriture di un ginepro.

The syntax is clunky and fails to capture Wilde's elegant, sensory prose.

The tone fails to capture the formal, Victorian register of Holmes and Watson.

results.translated_text: Sono andato alla foresta perché desideravo vivere deliberatamente, guardando solo i fatti essenziali della vita, e vedere se non avrei potuto imparare ciò che aveva da insegnare, e scoprire, quando sarei venuto a morire, di aver davvero vissuto. Non desideravo vivere ciò che non era vita: la vita è così cara; né desideravo praticare la rinuncia, a meno che non fosse assolutamente necessario. results.translated_text: Chiamami Ishmael. Alcuni anni fa—non importa esattamente quante volte, dato che non avevo denaro nel mio taschino e nient'altro particolare per interessarmi sulla riva, pensai di andare in giro un po' di più e vedere la parte acquatica del mondo. È un modo che ho per scacciar la spleen e regolare la circolazione. Ogni volta che mi trovo a diventare serio attorno alla bocca; ogni volta che è un novembre umido e piovoso nella mia anima, considero alto il momento di raggiungere il mare appena possibile.

results.translated_text: Fui para o bosque porque desejava viver com propósito, encarando apenas os fatos essenciais da vida e ver se não podia aprender aquilo que ela tinha a ensinar, e, quando chegasse à morte, não descobrir que nunca houvera vivido. results.translated_text: Chama-me Içmael. É uma maneira que tenho de expurgar o desalento e regular a circulação. Sempre que me vejo tornar sombrio à boca; sempre que é uma nebulosa outubre húmido e gotejante na minha alma, julgo chegar a ser alta hora de ir ao mar assim que puder.

results.translated_text: 我步入荒野，因为我想过一种深思熟虑的生活，只直面生活的基本事实，看看能否从中领悟其所要教给我的道理，以免当我最终走向死亡时，才发现自己未曾真正活过。我不愿过一种并非生命本身的生活，生命是如此宝贵；我也不愿轻易妥协认命，除非实在别无选择。我渴望活得深刻，汲取生活的全部精髓。

scores.feedback: The translation is excellent, capturing the philosophical depth and poetic rhythm of Thoreau's prose with natural, evocative Chinese phrasing. While 'woods' is translated as '荒野' (wilderness/wilds) rather than the more literal '森林' (forest), it effectively conveys the spirit of Thoreau's isolation.

results.translated_text: 叫我以实玛利吧。那是几年前的事情了——姑且不问究竟过了多久——我囊中空空，一无所有，对岸上的一切都提不起半点兴趣，便想航海去四处走走，看看这水上的世界。这是我排遣忧郁、调理气血的方法。每当我觉得嘴角隐隐发冷，或是感到灵魂中降下那种阴冷潮湿的十一月雨时，我就认定该是尽早出海的时候了。

scores.feedback: The translation is highly accurate and flows naturally in Chinese, successfully capturing the contemplative and melancholic tone of the original. While the translation of 'spleen' as '忧郁' (melancholy) is standard, it slightly misses the archaic physiological nuance of the original, but the overall literary quality remains excellent.

results.translated_text: 凡是有房產、又單身漢者，人必信其亟思求婚。然一男子初至某地，其心迹意圖無論旁人如何不得而詳，該地居民總以爲他非某家小姐莫屬。

scores.feedback: The translation adopts a classical, elegant literary style that suits Austen's tone well. While '有房產' (possessing real estate) is a slightly narrow interpretation of 'good fortune' (which implies wealth/status more broadly), the translation captures the ironic, formal voice of the original effectively.

results.translated_text: 工作室裡瀰漫著玫瑰的濃香，當輕柔的夏日微風在花園樹梢間吹拂，透過敞開的門扉，便傳來丁香那濃郁的氣味，或是野蔷薇那種更為淡雅的芬芳。他慣於躺在那張放有波斯鞍袋的沙發角落，抽吸著無數支香菸，而就在這時，他總能瞥見金雀花那蜜甜且蜜黃的花瓣輝光。

scores.feedback: Failed to parse evaluation response

results.translated_text: 「你顯然是曾身處阿富汗，我看得出來。」「究竟怎麼會這樣？」我不禁詫異。他自笑一聲說：「別提了。」接著道：「現在的問題是關於血紅蛋白。我想你明白這個發現的意義吧？」「在化學上這固然有趣，」我答道，「但就實際應用而言——」「喂，老弟，這可是近年來最具有實用價值的法醫發現啊！你難道看不出，它提供了檢驗血漬的萬無一失的方法嗎？」

scores.feedback: The translation is highly readable and captures the tone well, though 'Never mind' is slightly mistranslated as '別提了' (don't mention it) rather than the intended '不重要/別管這個' (it's not important), and 'Why, man' is slightly over-localized as '喂，老弟'.

results.translated_text: 我進入森林，是為了有意識地生活，只面對生活中最基本的事實，並試著了解它能教導我們什麼，以免臨死時發現自己未曾真正活過。我不願意過那種本非生活的日子，生命是如此珍貴；我也不願輕易接受現狀，除非絕對必要。我想要深刻地生活，將生命精華盡數吸取。

scores.feedback: The translation is excellent, capturing the philosophical depth and poetic rhythm of the original text with natural, elegant Traditional Chinese phrasing. It maintains the contemplative tone perfectly, though 'practise resignation' is slightly interpreted rather than literal, which is appropriate for the literary context.

results.translated_text: 叫我以實瑪利。數年前——不必過問究竟過了多久——我囊中空空，一無所有，岸上亦無特別令我感興趣之事，心想不妨出海遊歷一番，看看這水上的世界。這是我驅散愁悶、調理血脈的一法。每当我覺出口氣漸沉、面色鐵青之際；每逢我的靈魂裡籠罩著陰濕的十一月雨幕，我便認為此刻正是盡速駛向大海的時候了。

scores.feedback: The translation captures the melancholic and reflective tone of Melville's prose effectively, using appropriate literary Chinese phrasing. The rendering of 'damp, drizzly November in my soul' is particularly evocative and well-adapted.

results.translated_text: 大抵、財を成した単独の男性は、必ず妻を求めると言えよう。しかしその男性が初めから近隣の住民に受け入れられるかどうかについては誰も知らないとしても、周囲の家族の心中においては彼は誰かの娘の正当な所有物であるとみなされていることは確実である。

scores.feedback: The translation captures the basic meaning but fails to convey the iconic, ironic tone of Austen's opening; it reads more like a dry, modern report than 19th-century literature.

results.translated_text: スタジオはバラの豊かで濃厚な香りで満ちており、庭の木々の間に吹き抜ける光る初夏の風を伴って、開かれた戸からユリの重く濃厚な香りや、より繊細な香水のように広がるピンク色の花のトゲの香りが漂ってまいりました。ペルシャの鞍袋の上に横たわり、いつものように無数のタバコを吸いながら座っているロード・ヘンリー・ウォットンが、ただひときわはちみつ蜜のような甘さや色合いを持つリンデンの花の花びらの光りだけを角からくわえて見ていました。

scores.feedback: The translation suffers from significant errors, such as misinterpreting 'pink-flowering thorn' as 'thorny plant' and 'laburnum' as 'linden', while the final sentence is syntactically confused and fails to convey the original meaning.

results.translated_text: 「あなたはアフガニスタン到过ったと察しましたね」「えっ、いったいどうやってわかったんですか」と私は驚いて尋ねた。「そんなこと気にしなさい」彼は自ずから笑って言った。「今はヘモグロビンについて話しましょう。私のこの発見の重要性には気づいていますよね？」「化学的には面白いと思いますよ」私は答え、「でも実用的には─」「まあ、人間め、数年来もっとも実用性の高い法医学的发现ですよ。血液の痕跡を検出する決定的な方法が提供してくれると思いませんか？」

scores.feedback: The translation contains significant grammatical errors (e.g., '到过った', '发现' instead of '発見') and unnatural phrasing that fails to capture the Victorian tone of the original dialogue. The translation of 'Why, man' as 'まあ、人間め' is particularly jarring and inaccurate in this context.

results.translated_text: 森へ入ったのは、まことに生きているというように暮らそうと、生命の本質的事実だけを前に立ち向かい、学びうることを得ようと、そして死に臨むとき、「自分は生きたことがなかった」と悟りたくないからであった。私は虚偽の生を送ろうとしたわけではない；生はあまりに愛おしいからである。また必要以外の苦渋を強要する気もなかったのである。私は深く生きたいし、生命の髄をすべて吸い取ろうと思ったのである。

scores.feedback: The translation captures the philosophical essence well, though some phrasing (like 'まことに生きているというように') feels slightly clunky compared to the poetic precision of the original. It successfully conveys the gravity and intent of Thoreau's famous passage.

results.translated_text: 私をイシュマエルと呼んでください。数年前のことです（正確にはどれくらい前かは問わないでください）。ポケットにお金があまりなく、陸上でも特に興味を持つべきことのない私が、少し海上に出て世界の水辺を見て回ろうと思いました。これは私の脾臓を取り除き循環を調整する方法なのです。いつも口元が不気味になり始めたり、私の中での十一月が湿り気を帯びてぐずついている時 whenever it is a damp, drizzly November in my soul; すぐに海に出ないとだめだと考えるのです。

scores.feedback: The translation fails to capture the archaic, literary tone of Melville, opting for a modern, literal approach that misses the poetic nuance. The inclusion of untranslated English text in the final sentence is a significant error, and the translation of 'spleen' as a literal organ rather than the archaic 'melancholy' misinterprets the source.

results.translated_text: 재산을 보유하고 있는 남자가 아내를 원해야 한다는 것은 전 인류가 널리 인정하는 진리다. 그러나 어떤 남자가 이웃으로 들어섰을 때 그의 감정이든 관점이든 그토록 알려지지 않았다고 하더라도, 주변의 가족들은 이 진리가 너무도 확고히 마음에 각인되어 있어서 그가 그들의 딸들 중 한 명 혹은 다른 한 명의 정당한 소유물로 간주한다고 생각한다.

scores.feedback: The translation is literal and captures the basic meaning, but it fails to convey the famous ironic, rhythmic cadence of Austen's prose. The Korean phrasing is stiff and unnatural, particularly in the second sentence, where the sentence structure becomes convoluted and difficult to follow.

results.translated_text: 스튜디오는 장미의 풍부한 향기로 가득 찼으며, 정원 나무 사이를 휘둘러대는 밝은 여름 바람이 지나가자 문으로 들어온 것은 계곡의 무거운 향기 혹은 분홍 꽃잎이 피어 있는 가시가 내는 더 섬세한 향기였다. 그는 관습에 따라 연거푸 담배를 피우며 누워있던 터키 안락의자의 코너에서, 금빛 달콤함 그리고 금빛 색조의 박람꽃의 빛을 딱딱금 잡을 수 있었다.

scores.feedback: The translation contains significant errors, most notably mistranslating 'lilac' (라일락) as 'valley' (계계곡), 'laburnum' (금작화) as 'exhibition flower' (박람꽃), and 'Persian saddle-bags' as 'Turkish armchair'. The phrasing is awkward and unnatural, failing to capture the aesthetic, sensory elegance of Wilde's prose.

results.translated_text: "아프가니스탄에 다녀오셨나 보군요." "어떻게 그런걸 알았어요?" 저는 놀라면서도 물었다. 그는 혼자 웃음을 터뜨리며 말했다. "그건 다 부치세요. 지금 중요한 것은 헤모글로빈이죠. 내 발견의 의의를 아시겠습니까?" "물론 화학적으로 흥미롭지만, 실용적으로—" 그가 말을 이었다. "허어, 친구야. 이건 몇 년 동안 가장 실용적인 법의학 발견이야. 핏자국을 찾는 데 확실한 판별법이 안겨주지 않나?"

scores.feedback: The translation captures the core meaning well, though '그건 다 부치세요' is an unnatural translation of 'Never mind' in this context. The tone is generally appropriate for Holmes and Watson, but some phrasing feels slightly stiff or archaic in a way that doesn't quite match the original's crisp Victorian dialogue.

results.translated_text: 나는 고독한 삶을 위해, 삶이 우리에게 가르쳐 줄 것을 배워볼 수 있는 기회를 얻기 원했고, 죽을 때가 되어 살아보지 못했을 걸 후회하지 않으려고 나무 속으로 들어가 살았다. 나는 삶의 본질적인 사실들을 마주하며 살아보고자 했고, 죽음 앞에 서서 내가 살아본 것이 아니었음을 발견하지 않으려 했던 것이다. 나는 죽은 삶이 아니라 진짜 삶을 살고자 했다. 삶이란 너무나 귀중한데도. 또한, 꼭 필요할 때만이라면 예외로 할 수 없으나, 그렇지 않다면 체념의 관행에는 참여하고 싶지 않았다. 나는 깊고 뿌리 깊은 삶을 살며, 삶의 결을 모두 흡수해 보고자 했다.

scores.feedback: The translation takes significant liberties with the original syntax and meaning, particularly in the first sentence where 'deliberately' is mistranslated as 'lonely/solitary' (고독한). While the Korean flows reasonably well, it loses the philosophical precision and iconic rhythm of Thoreau's prose.

results.translated_text: 나를 이시마엘이라 부름은 하겠다. 몇 해 전, 돈은 하나도 없어지고 마음에는 별것 없는 것이라, 나는 세계의 물기가 스미는 곳 좀 돌아다니며 보고자 하고 생각했다. 그것은 나의 기분을 풀고 혈류를 조절하는 방법이다. 입술이 검어질 때마다, 내 마음에 습하고 비린 11월이 찾아올 때마다, 내가 곧 바다로 가기를 기다릴 때라고 생각한다.

scores.feedback: The translation struggles to capture the archaic, rhythmic cadence of Melville's prose, often resulting in awkward phrasing (e.g., '나를 이시마엘이라 부름은 하겠다'). While the literal meaning is generally conveyed, the translation fails to evoke the literary gravitas and specific tone of the original.

results.translated_text: Đó là một chân lý được công nhận ở khắp mọi nơi: rằng một người đàn ông độc thân có nhiều tiền, ắt hẳn cần tìm vợ cho mình. Tuy nhiên, dù cảm xúc hay quan điểm của người đàn ông ấy khi lần đầu đến vùng đất mới có thể chưa ai thấu hiểu thì sự thật đó đã được khắc sâu trong tâm trí của những gia đình xung quanh đến mức họ đều xem xét rằng chính một hoặc hai cô con gái của họ là thuộc tính hợp pháp của người này.

scores.feedback: The translation captures the literal meaning well, but the final clause 'thuộc tính hợp pháp' is a mistranslation of 'rightful property,' which should imply the man is the property of the daughter, not vice versa. The tone is overly literal and lacks the sharp, ironic wit characteristic of Austen's original prose.

results.translated_text: Buồng ở ngập tràn hương thơm nồng nàn của hoa hồng; và khi luồng gió mùa hè nhẹ nhàng vi vu giữa các tán cây trong vườn, thì qua cửa mở ra, một mùi hương nặng nề của hoa oải giàng hay một thứ hương tinh khiết hơn của bụi gai nở hoa màu hồng đã xộc vào. Từ góc chiếc ghế ba gian kiểu Ba Tư mà anh ta đang nằm đó, hút thuốc lá như thói quen, những điếu thuốc vô số của bá tước Henry Wotton, ngài chỉ vừa kịp catch được cái ánh sáng vàng ươm và màu mật ong của những bông hoa huỳnh huỳn.

scores.feedback: The translation suffers from significant errors, including the misuse of 'oải giàng' for lilac (tử đinh hương), the awkward retention of the English verb 'catch', and the misspelling of 'laburnum' (kim tước). While it attempts to capture the sensory details, the phrasing is clunky and lacks the refined, aesthetic tone of Wilde's original prose.

results.translated_text: "Bạn đã từng ở Afghanistan, tôi cảm nhận được." "Cao thế nào anh mới biết được vậy?" Tôi hỏi đầy kinh ngạc. "Thôi nào," ông ấy đáp, cười khà. "Điều cần bàn bây giờ là về hemoglobin. Anh không thấy rõ tầm quan trọng của khám phá này chăng?" "Về mặt hóa học thì thú vị lắm, dĩ nhiên," tôi trả lời, "nhưng về thực tiễn—" "Chao trời, nó chính là khám phá y pháp có giá trị thực tiễn nhất trong nhiều năm nay. Anh không thấy rằng nó mang lại cho chúng ta một phép thử tuyệt đối tin cậy để xác định vết máu sao?"

scores.feedback: The translation captures the core meaning well, but the tone is slightly off; 'Cao thế nào' is an unnatural translation of 'How on earth', and 'cười khà' feels too boisterous for Holmes's character, which is typically more restrained.

results.translated_text: Tôi đã vào rừng bởi tôi muốn sống một cách có chủ ý, để đối diện chỉ với những sự thật cốt yếu của đời, và xem liệu tôi có thể học được điều nó cần dạy, hay không, khi đến lúc phải chết, mới phát hiện ra mình chưa từng sống. Tôi không muốn sống những thứ không phải là cuộc sống; vì sống quý giá lắm; cũng như tôi không muốn cầu xin chịu đựng cho tới khi thật sự cần thiết. Tôi muốn sống sâu và hót hết髓 của đời.

scores.feedback: The translation captures the core meaning well, but suffers from awkward phrasing ('hót hết髓' is a mix of Vietnamese and Chinese characters, which is incorrect) and a lack of the poetic rhythm essential to Thoreau's prose.

results.translated_text: Gọi tôi là Ahab. Cách đây bao nhiêu năm rồi—cứ kệ chính xác là bao lâu—với túi hầu như không còn đồng nào, và trên đất liền chẳng có gì thú vị cả, tôi nghĩ rằng mình sẽ lượn một vòng và xem phần nước của thế giới này. Đây là cách tôi thường dùng để xua tan những bực bội trong lòng và điều tiết dòng chảy của máu. Bất cứ khi nào tôi thấy mình trở nên khắc khổ đến mức mép mồm; bất cứ khi nào trong tâm hồn tôi lại là tháng Mát-nhờ (November) ảm đạm, mưa phùn, thì tôi cho rằng đã đến lúc phải ra khơi nhanh chóng nhất có thể.

scores.feedback: The translation commits a critical error by changing the narrator's name from 'Ishmael' to 'Ahab', fundamentally altering the text's meaning. While the prose flows reasonably well, the tone is too colloquial ('lượn một vòng') for Melville's archaic, literary style.

results.translated_text: ถือเป็นเรื่องเป็นความจริงที่ทุกคนยอมรับกันว่า ผู้ชายโสดผู้มั่งมี ย่อมจะต้องต้องการภรรยาเสมอ อย่างไรดี ความรู้สึกหรือมุมมองของผู้ชายคนนั้นเมื่อครั้ง初入เข้าสู่ย่านชุมชนนั้นอาจเป็นที่รับรู้ไม่แพร่หลายสักปานใด แต่ความจริงอันนี้กลับฝังแน่นในความคิดของครอบครัวทั้งหลายโดยรอบจนกระทั่งเขาถูกมองว่าเป็นทรัพย์สินสิทธิ์ชอบธรรมของหญิงสาวคนหนึ่ง หรืออีกคนหนึ่งก็ได้จากบรรดาพวกธิดาของพวกเขา

scores.feedback: The translation is accurate in meaning, but the inclusion of the Chinese character '初' disrupts fluency. The phrasing is overly literal and lacks the elegant, ironic wit characteristic of Austen's prose, feeling more like a mechanical translation than a literary one.

results.translated_text: ในสตูดิโอซึ่งเต็มไปด้วยกลิ่นหอมฉุนของกุหลาบ และเมื่อลมฤดูร้อนที่อ่อนโยนพัดผ่านระหว่างต้นไม้ในสวน ก็ส่งกลิ่นหอมอันหนักแน่นของดอกมัลลิกา หรือกลิ่นอโรมาอันละมุนละไมของหนามสีชมพูทะลุช่องประตูเข้ามา ส่วนจากมุมของโซฟาเปอร์เซียที่เขาเอนตัวนอนอยู่พร้อมสูบบุหรี่ตาม癖ของเขา ลอร์ด เฮนรี ว็อตตัน มองเห็นเพียงแวบหนึ่งของกลีบดอกไม้สีทองหวานหอมและสีอ่อนคล้ายน้ำผึ้ง ของต้นเลปรันดัมที่อยู่ห่างออกไป

results.translated_text: É uma verdade universalmente reconhecida de que um solteiro, possuidor de uma boa fortuna, deve estar à procura de esposa. Por pouco conhecidos que sejam os sentimentos ou as opiniões de tal homem, logo que ingressa num bairro, essa verdade está tão firmemente implantada nas mentes das famílias vizinhas que ele é considerado propriedade legítima de alguém entre suas filhas.

scores.feedback: The translation is excellent, capturing the ironic tone and syntactic structure of Austen's prose with high fidelity. The slight awkwardness of 'de que' after 'reconhecida' is a minor grammatical quibble that does not detract from the overall elegance.

results.translated_text: O estúdio estava impregnado do odor denso das rosas e, quando a brisa de verão agitava as árvores do jardim, entrava pela porta aberta o perfume pesado da lilácea ou a fragrância mais sutil do espinheiro florescente rosa. Do canto do divã coberto por almofadas persas sobre o qual ele jazia, fumando, como era seu hábito, inumeráveis cigarros, Lord Henry Wotton conseguia vislumbrar o brilho das flores melosas e de cor dourada de um laburnum.

scores.feedback: The translation is excellent, capturing the sensory richness and aesthetic tone of Wilde's prose with natural-sounding Portuguese, though 'lilácea' is a slightly less common botanical term than 'lilás' for the flower.

results.translated_text: "O senhor esteve no Afeganistão, percebo." "Como diabos pôde o senhor saber disso?" perguntei eu, estupefato. "Deixe para lá", disse ele, rindo consigo mesmo. "A questão agora é sobre a hemoglobina. Certamente, vê a importância da minha descoberta?" "É interessante, quimicamente, sem dúvida", respondi eu, "mas na prática—" "Por que, homem, é a mais prática das descobertas médico-legais há anos. Não vê que ela nos fornece um teste infalível para manchas de sangue?"

scores.feedback: The translation is excellent, capturing the formal, Victorian tone of the original dialogue with natural-sounding Portuguese phrasing. The only minor critique is that 'Por que, homem' is a literal translation of 'Why, man,' which sounds slightly less idiomatic in Portuguese than the original English exclamation.

results.translated_text: Fui para o bosque porque desejava viver de forma deliberada, confrontar apenas os fatos essenciais da vida e ver se poderia aprender o que ela tinha para me ensinar, de modo que, quando chegasse a hora de morrer, não descobrisse que não tivera vivido. Não desejava viver o que não era vida, pois a vida é tão preciosa; nem queria praticar a resignação, a menos que fosse estritamente necessário. Queria viver profundamente e sugar todo o suco da vida.

scores.feedback: The translation is highly readable and accurate, though the final metaphor 'sugar todo o suco da vida' loses the visceral impact of Thoreau's original 'suck out all the marrow', which is better rendered as 'tutano' in Portuguese.

results.translated_text: Chame-me Ishmael. Alguns anos atrás — não importa há quanto tempo exatamente, sem quase nem nenhum dinheiro na minha bolsa e nada de particular que me interessasse em terra, decidi navegar um pouco para ver a parte aquática do mundo. É assim que eu costumo afastar o mau humor e regular a circulação. Sempre que sinto a boca se fechando em uma expressão sombria; sempre que dentro da alma há um novembro úmido e chuvoso, considero ser alta hora de zarpar logo que possível.

scores.feedback: The translation is accurate in meaning but struggles to capture the rhythmic, archaic cadence of Melville's prose, particularly in the clunky phrasing of 'sem quase nem nenhum dinheiro' and 'alta hora'. It conveys the narrative well but lacks the poetic gravitas of the original.

results.translated_text: Jest to prawda powszechnie uznawana, że samotny mężczyzna posiadający spory majątek musi brakować żony. Choćby jego uczucia lub poglądy były jak najmniej znane w chwili pierwszego pojawienia się w okolicy, prawda ta jest tak głęboko zakorzeniona w umysłach otaczających go rodzin, że uważany jest on za wyłączną własność którejś z ich córek – czy to tej, czy tamtej.

scores.feedback: The translation is generally accurate, but the phrase 'musi brakować żony' is grammatically incorrect in Polish (it should be 'musi potrzebować żony' or 'musi odczuwać brak żony'). The stylistic tone captures Austen's irony well, though the added phrase '– czy to tej, czy tamtej' is an unnecessary embellishment not present in the original.

results.translated_text: Pomieszczenie studiowe wypełniał bogaty zapach róż, a gdy letnie wiatry poruszały się wśród drzew ogrodu, przez otwarte drzwi wnikał ciężki zapach bezu lub bardziej subtelny perfumowany aromat kolca z różowymi kwiatami. Z kąta divanu przykrytego perskimi siodełkami, na którym leżał paląc, zgodnie ze swoim zwyczajem, niezliczone papierosy, lord Henry Wotton mógł dostrzec jedynie błysk złotych, miodowo-słodkich i miodowo-żółtych kwiatów laburnum.

scores.feedback: The translation is generally accurate but suffers from awkward phrasing (e.g., 'pomieszczenie studiowe', 'perfumowany aromat') and literal translations that clash with Polish literary norms. It captures the meaning well but lacks the elegant, evocative flow of Wilde's original prose.

results.translated_text: „Był pan w Afganistanie, domyślam się." „Jakże mógł pan o tym wiedzieć?" zapytałem zdumiony. „Nie ma znaczenia", odpowiedział, śmiejąc się do siebie. „Obecnie chodzi o hemoglobinę. Nie wątpić, że Pan rozumie znaczenie mojego odkrycia? Jest ono interesujące, chemicznie rzecz biorąc, bez wątpienia" odrzekłem, „ale praktycznie..." „Cóż, człowieku, to najbardziej praktyczne lekarstwo w dziedzinie medycyny sądowej od lat. Czy pan nie dostrzega, że daje nam ono niewadliwe badanie plam krwi?"

scores.feedback: The translation suffers from significant errors, particularly 'Nie wątpić' (grammatically incorrect) and the mistranslation of 'medico-legal discovery' as 'lekarstwo' (medicine/cure instead of discovery/finding). While the dialogue flow is recognizable, the awkward phrasing and grammatical mistakes detract from the professional tone of the original.

results.translated_text:
<TRANSLA>
Poszedłem do lasu, ponieważ pragnąłem żyć świadomie, zmierzyć się z samymi istotnymi faktami życia i przekonać się, czy nie mogę nauczyć się czegoś od niego, a gdy przyjdzie śmierć, nie odkryć, że nie żyłem. Nie chciałem żyć czymś, co nie jest życiem; życie jest zbyt cenne, by je marnować. Nie zamierzałem też praktykować rezygnacji, chyba że byłoby to absolutnie konieczne. Pragnąłem żyć głęboko i wyssać z niego wszystkie soki.
</TRANSLAT>

scores.feedback: The translation is highly readable and captures the philosophical tone well, though 'suck out all the marrow' is idiomatic and slightly softened to 'wyssać wszystkie soki' (suck out all the juices), losing a bit of the original's visceral intensity.

results.translated_text: Zwano mnie Ishmaelem. Kilka lat temu – nie pytajcie, ile dokładnie – mając bardzo mało pieniędzy w portfelu i żadnych konkretnych powodów, by interesować się lądem, pomyślałem, że wypłynę na morze, żeby zobaczyć wodną część świata. Tak to już mam: gdy gnębi mnie melancholia i gdy w duszy szarzeje jak wilgotny, deszczowy listopad, uważam, że najwyższa pora ruszyć w morze.

scores.feedback: The translation is highly readable and captures the essence well, though 'Zwano mnie Ishmaelem' (They called me Ishmael) misses the iconic, imperative invitation of the original 'Call me Ishmael'. The stylistic rendering of 'damp, drizzly November' is poetic and effective in Polish.

results.translated_text: 凡是有钱的单身汉，总想娶位太太，这已经成了一条举世公认的真理。尽管他刚搬到一个新地方时，他的感情或想法很少有人知道，但这真理在当地居民的心目中却根深蒂固，以至于人们都认为，他不是某一家女儿的合法财产，就是另一家的。

scores.feedback: This is a classic, highly regarded translation that captures the ironic tone and precise meaning of the original with excellent natural flow in Chinese.

results.translated_text: 室内弥漫着浓郁的玫瑰香气，当夏日微风轻拂花园中的树丛，透过敞开的门，飘来丁香沉郁的芬芳，或是开粉红花的刺玫那更 delicate 的清香。从铺着波斯鞍袋的长沙发一角——他正躺在那里，照例不停地抽着香烟——勋爵亨利·沃顿勉强能瞥见那株金链花上金黄如蜜、色泽如蜜的花朵闪动着微光。

scores.feedback: The translation is generally accurate and captures the imagery well, but the inclusion of the untranslated English word 'delicate' disrupts the fluency and literary tone. Additionally, '勋爵亨利·沃顿' is a slightly awkward rendering of 'Lord Henry Wotton', which is more conventionally translated as '亨利·沃顿勋爵'.

results.translated_text: "您去过阿富汗，我看得出来。" "您究竟是如何得知的？"我惊讶地问道。"且慢，"他一边自言自语地笑着，一边说道，"现在要谈的是血红蛋白。想必您已明白我这一发现的意义所在？""从化学角度看，这确实很有趣，"我回答，"但实用方面——" "天哪，先生，这是近年来最实用的法医学发现。难道您没看到，它为血迹检测提供了无可置疑的验证吗？"

scores.feedback: The translation is highly accurate and captures the Victorian tone of the original dialogue well. The rendering of 'Never mind' as '且慢' is a slight stylistic interpretation, but it fits the context of Holmes's dismissive yet enigmatic character effectively.

results.translated_text: 我走进森林，因为我希望生活得有意义，直面生命中本质的事实，看看能否从中学到它要传授的智慧，以免在临终时才发现自己从未真正活过。我不愿虚度光阴，因为生命如此珍贵；我也无意刻意忍耐，除非万不得已。我希望深深地活着，吸尽生命的所有精华。

scores.feedback: The translation is excellent, capturing the philosophical depth and poetic rhythm of the original with natural, elegant Chinese phrasing. It slightly simplifies 'deliberately' to '有意义' (meaningful) rather than '深思熟虑' (deliberate/thoughtful), but this fits the context well.

results.translated_text: 叫我以实玛利吧。多年前——不必深究究竟多久了——我 purse 里没钱，岸上也没什么特别吸引我的事，于是便想出海转转，去看看那水的世界。这是我驱散忧郁、调节循环的惯用法子。每当我觉得嘴角紧绷；每当我的灵魂如湿冷阴沉的十一月般萧瑟，我便觉得该赶紧出海才是。

scores.feedback: The translation is accurate and readable, but the inclusion of the English word 'purse' disrupts the literary tone, and the phrasing lacks the archaic, rhythmic gravitas expected of Melville's prose.

results.translated_text: 凡是有錢的單身漢，必想娶太太，這是一條天下公認的真理。無論此人的心情或意見在初到一個地方時是如何不為人知，但這項真理卻為周圍的家庭所深信不疑，認為他理所當然地是他們某個女兒的囊中之物。

scores.feedback: The translation is excellent, capturing the ironic tone and precise meaning of Austen's prose with natural, elegant Traditional Chinese phrasing.

results.translated_text: 工作室裡瀰漫著薔薇的馥郁芬芳，當夏日微風吹拂園中樹木時，透過敞開的門扉，便能聞到紫丁香的濃郁氣息，或是薔薇狀荊棘那更清雅的馨香。哈里·沃頓爵士躺在波斯鞍袋式軟榻的一角，正像他向來習慣般地吸著無數支香煙；從那角度望去，僅能瞥見一株黃金櫸樹綻放的花簇，那花朵如蜜般甜美，又呈現出蜂蜜般的色澤。

scores.feedback: The translation is highly accurate and captures the aesthetic, sensory-rich tone of Wilde's prose effectively. The phrasing is elegant and natural in Traditional Chinese, though '薔薇狀荊棘' for 'pink-flowering thorn' is a slightly literal botanical translation that could be smoother.

results.translated_text:
「你曾去過阿富汗，我想我已經察覺到了。」
「你究竟是如何知道這一點的？」我驚訝地問道。
「且不用多言，」他自顧自地笑道，「現在的問題是關於血紅素。你不認為我這個發現具有重要意義嗎？」
「從化學角度來看，這確實饒有趣味，」我答道，「但在實際應用上——」
「哎呀，先生，這是近年來最實用的法醫學發現了。你難道看不出它能為我們提供一個識別血漬的無誤方法嗎？」

scores.feedback: The translation is highly accurate and captures the Victorian tone and character dynamics well. The phrasing is natural and flows smoothly in Traditional Chinese, maintaining the intellectual yet slightly arrogant voice of Holmes.

results.translated_text: 我去到林中，是希望過得審慎 deliberate（深思熟慮地生活），直面生命最根本的事實，看我能否學到它要教導的一切；以免在臨終之時，發現自己其實從未真正活過。我不願生活在虛妄之中，因為生命是如此珍貴；也不願習於妥協，除非迫不得已。我渴望活得深透，將生命的精髓一一吮吸出來。

scores.feedback: The translation captures the philosophical depth and poetic tone of Thoreau well, though the inclusion of the English word 'deliberate' in parentheses disrupts the literary flow. Otherwise, the phrasing is elegant and accurately conveys the original's contemplative spirit.

results.translated_text: 叫我以實瑪利。數年前——不問究竟多長——囊中無幾何銀兩，陸上亦無特別興味之事讓我心動，我便想著乘舟遠航，稍作遊歷，見識那遍海之世界。這是我排遣鬱悶、調節氣血的一種法子。每當我覺嘴唇緊抿，眉宇間染上淒涼；每當我靈魂中如同身處濕冷陰沉的十一月，我便覺得早該盡快出海為好。

scores.feedback: The translation captures the archaic, contemplative tone of Melville's prose with elegant, classical-leaning Chinese phrasing. It successfully balances literal meaning with the specific literary rhythm of the original.

results.translated_text: 富を持つ未婚の男は、必ずしも妻を欲しているという真理は、世間で広く認められている。たとえその男が近所へやって来たとき、その感情や意見についてほとんど知られていようとも、この真理は周囲の家族たちの心の中で強く定着しており、彼はいずれかの娘 rightful property であるとみなされている。

scores.feedback: The translation contains a critical error in the first sentence, stating the opposite of the original meaning (saying a rich man *doesn't necessarily* want a wife). Additionally, the inclusion of the English phrase 'rightful property' disrupts the flow, and the overall tone fails to capture Austen's sophisticated, ironic prose.

results.translated_text: 部屋にはバラの豊潤な香りが満ち、庭の樹木を軽やかな夏風がゆさぶるたびに、開かれた戸を通してスミレ色のラベンダーの濃厚な香りや、ピンクの花を咲かせるトゲトガリの繊細な匂いが届いた。彼はペルシア製の鞍型敷物に横たわり、常として無数のシガレットを吸いながら、そのコーナーから黄金色の蜜のような甘さを放つユリノキの花が輝くのがわずかに見えた。

scores.feedback: The translation contains significant botanical errors ('lilac' translated as 'lavender', 'thorn' as 'toge-togari', 'laburnum' as 'tulip tree'), which distort the specific aesthetic imagery Wilde intended. While the prose flows reasonably well in Japanese, these factual inaccuracies undermine the literary fidelity of the passage.

results.translated_text: 「あなたはアフガニスタンの経験がありますね」と私は言われた。「どうしてそれをご存知なのですか？」と私は驚いて尋ねた。「それは後にするにせよ」と彼は自分自身で笑いを噛み殺しながら言った。「今問われているのはヘモグロビンのことだ。私発見の意義がお分かりではないか？」「化学的には確かに興味深い発見ですが、実用的には——」「いや、君よ。これは長年ない medici-legal 分野での最も実用的な発見だ。血痕に対する確実な判定法が得られることをお分かりになるだろう？」

scores.feedback: The translation captures the core meaning but suffers from awkward phrasing (e.g., 'medici-legal' left untranslated) and a tone that feels slightly stiff compared to the original Victorian dialogue. The dialogue attribution 'と私は言われた' is also clunky compared to standard Japanese literary conventions.

results.translated_text: 私は人生を徹底して生きようと思い、森に行きました。ただ、人生の本質的な事実と向き合い、それが何を教えるか学び、そして死ぬ際に自分が生きてこなかったことに気づかないようにするためです。私は、死んでしまったら生きることではないような人生を生きるつもりはありませんでした。生はあまりにも尊いのです。また、全く必要がない限り、安易な諦念を実践することも望みませんでした。私は深く生きようとし、人生の髄まで絞り取ろうとしたのです。

scores.feedback: The translation is highly readable and captures the core meaning well, though it slightly softens the poetic intensity of Thoreau's original prose, particularly in the rendering of 'suck out all the marrow'.

results.translated_text: 私をイシュメエルと呼んでくれ。何年か前のことだ——正確にはいくつ前にだったかは問わない——財布の中身がほとんどなく、陸で興味を引かれるものも何もなかった私は、少し航海をして水の多い世界の様子を見てやろうと考えたのだ。これは私が憂い払い、血行を整えるための方法である。私の口元がついに暗く曇った時、あるいは私の魂が湿り気あり、霧雨が降る十一月である時には、すぐにでも船出をするようこそ、その頃になると考えるのである。

scores.feedback: While the translation captures the literal meaning, it struggles with the archaic, rhythmic tone of Melville's prose, particularly in the final sentence which becomes convoluted and grammatically awkward in Japanese.

results.translated_text: 모든 세상에 널리 알려진 진리는, 막대한 재산을 소유한 독신 남자는 반드시 아내를 원하는 것이라는 사실이다. 그가 이웃에 새로 이사하여 들어올 때 그의 감정이나 의견이 얼마나 알려지지 않았는지 간과할 수는 있으나, 이 진리는 주변 가문의 머리 속에 너무 단단히 박혀 있어 그 남자는 곧 그들의 딸들 중 누구의 권리로 소유되어야 할 당연한 재산으로 여겨진다.

scores.feedback: The translation is accurate in meaning, but the phrasing is somewhat stiff and overly literal, failing to capture the sharp, ironic wit of Austen's original prose. The Korean sentence structure feels slightly clunky, particularly in the second sentence, which lacks the elegant flow of the classic opening.

results.translated_text: 스튜디오는 장미의 향연이 가득했고, 여름 햇살 아래 정원의 나무들 사이로 미풍이 부니 열린 문 너머로 아편꽃 같은 라일락의 무거운 향이나, 꽃을 피운 가시나무의 섬세한 향기가 밀려왔다. 그는 페르시아식 안자루 소파의 구석에 누워 있어 흡연 중이었다. 그 역시 평상처럼 담배를 한 대 이어 한 대 피우며, 그의 이름은 헨리 와튼 경이었다. 금빛으로 달콤하고 꿀색을 머금은 로라니아 꽃이 창가를 비추는 듯했다.

scores.feedback: The translation suffers from significant errors, including mistranslating 'lilac' as 'opium flower' (아편꽃) and misinterpreting the sentence structure regarding Lord Henry's actions and identity. The flow is disjointed, failing to capture the lush, aesthetic prose of Wilde's original text.

results.translated_text: "아프가니스탄에 계셨군요." "도대체 어떻게 아셨습니까?"라고 나는 놀라며 물었다. "그건 이제 그만두고," 그가 혼자서 웃음을 터뜨리며 말했다. "이제 문제는 헤모글로빈입니다. 내가 이 discoveries 의 중요성을 깨닫지 않으시겠습니까?" "화학적으로는 흥미롭군요, 물론 실용적 측면에서는—" "아이고, 그 친구여, 이건 수년 동안 나온 가장 실용적인 medico-legal 발견입니다. 피 얼룩에 대한 절대 신뢰할 수 있는 검사 방법이 생긴 것이라고 보지 않소?"

scores.feedback: The translation suffers from jarring code-switching (using English words 'discoveries' and 'medico-legal' in the Korean text), which ruins the literary immersion. Additionally, the translation of 'Why, man' as '아이고, 그 친구여' is unnatural and overly colloquial/clunky for the Victorian tone.

results.translated_text: 나는 의도적으로 살아보기 위해 숲으로 들어갔다. 삶의 본질적인 사실들 앞에서 정면으로 마주하고, 그로부터 배우려 했으며, 죽음이 찾아왔을 때 내가 살아보지 못했음을 발견하지 않으려 했다. 나는 삶이 아닌 것을 살고 싶지 않았다. 삶은 그렇게 귀하기 때문이다. 또한 필요하지 않은 한 체념의 자세를 취하려 하지도 않았다. 나는 깊이 살아내고자 했고, 삶의 모든 골수를 빨아들였다.

scores.feedback: The translation is excellent, capturing the philosophical depth and poetic rhythm of the original text with natural, sophisticated Korean phrasing. The final sentence is particularly well-rendered, maintaining the visceral metaphor of 'sucking out the marrow' effectively.

results.translated_text: 나를 이쉬멜이라고 불러다오. 몇 년 전에—얼마 전인지는 정확히 말해 볼 필요도 없이—주머니에 돈이 거의 없었고 육지에서 나를 흥미롭게 할 것도 없었기에, 나는 잠시 배를 타고 바다를 돌아다니며 이 세상의 물리적 세계를 구경해 보기로 결정했다. 이는 내가 우울증을 털어내고 순환을 조절하는 나름의 방식이다. 입가가 항상 굳은 듯하고 내 영혼이 11월의 축축하고 비오는 날처럼 감겨 있을 때면, 나는 곧바로 배를 타고 항해를 시작해야 할 때가 되었다고 여긴다.

scores.feedback: The translation captures the literal meaning well, but misses the archaic, rhythmic cadence of Melville's prose, particularly in the translation of 'watery part of the world' and 'spleen,' which feel too modern and clinical in Korean.

results.translated_text: Đó là một sự thật phổ quát rằng một người đàn ông độc thân có một khối tài sản kha khá thì tất phải cần tìm một cô vợ. Tuy hiểu biết của anh ta về cảm xúc hay quan điểm của bản thân khi mới bước vào một khu vực xung quanh có thể rất ít ỏi, nhưng chân lý ấy đã được khắc sâu trong tâm trí của những gia đình lân cận đến mức anh ta được xem là tài sản chính đáng của một hoặc vài cô gái nào đó trong số họ.

scores.feedback: The translation is highly accurate and captures the meaning well, though it slightly loses the sharp, ironic wit of Austen's original syntax. The phrasing is grammatically sound and natural in Vietnamese, though slightly more literal than the iconic, rhythmic cadence of the English opening.

results.translated_text: Buồng khách phòng đầy hương thơm ngào ngạt của hoa hồng, và khi gió mùa hè nhẹ nhàng len lỏi qua những hàng cây trong khu vườn, từ cánh cửa mở ra đã thoảng đến mùi hương nồng nàn của hoa oải hương hay chút thoang thoảng tinh tế hơn của loại gai nở hoa màu hồng. Từ góc ghế trường kỷ trải khăn lông ngựa Ba Tư nơi Ngài đang nằm, hút thuốc như thói quen thường ngày vô số điếu thuốc lá, Lord Henry Wotton chỉ kịp vịn thoáng glimpse những bông hoa vàng óng mật ong rực rỡ và mang sắc vàng mật ong rực rỡ của cây kim ngân.

scores.feedback: The translation suffers from significant botanical mistranslations (lilac as 'oải hương'/lavender, laburnum as 'kim ngân'/honeysuckle) and awkward phrasing, particularly the clumsy insertion of English words ('glimpse') and repetitive, unnatural sentence structure.

results.translated_text: "Tôi thấy rằng ngài từng đến Afghanistan," "Thật sao, làm thế nào ngài biết điều ấy?" tôi hỏi đầy kinh ngạc. "Không cần nói vậy nữa," ông ta vừa cười nhỏ vừa đáp. "Câu hỏi giờ đây là về hemoglobin. Không nghi ngờ gì nữa, ngài thấy tầm quan trọng của phát hiện này chứ?" "Điều đó thú vị về mặt hóa học, không ai tranh cãi," tôi trả lời, "nhưng thực tế—" "Này bác sĩ, đó chính là phát hiện y - tư pháp thực tiễn nhất trong nhiều năm qua. Ngài có thấy rằng nó mang lại cho ta một phương pháp kiểm tra vết máu tuyệt đối chính xác?"

scores.feedback: The translation is accurate and flows well, though the rendering of 'How on earth' as 'Thật sao' loses some of the original's emphatic surprise, and 'Không cần nói vậy nữa' is a slightly awkward translation of 'Never mind'.

results.translated_text: Tôi vào rừng bởi tôi ao ước được sống chủ đích, đối diện trực tiếp với những thực tại cốt yếu của cuộc đời, và xem liệu mình có thể học được bài học nó mang lại hay không, chứ không phải khi đến lúc chết đi mới nhận ra rằng mình chưa từng sống thật sự. Tôi chẳng muốn sống một thứ không phải là sự sống, bởi lẽ sự sống quá đỗi trân quý; cũng chẳng nguyện cam chịu, trừ khi thực sự cần thiết. Tôi muốn sống sâu sắc và hút cạn hết tủy sống của cuộc đời.

scores.feedback: The translation is excellent, capturing the philosophical depth and poetic tone of Thoreau's prose with natural, evocative Vietnamese phrasing. The translation of 'suck out all the marrow of life' as 'hút cạn hết tủy sống của cuộc đời' is particularly strong and faithful to the original imagery.

results.translated_text: Hãy gọi tôi là Ishmael. Cách đây một thời gian—không cần biết chính xác bao lâu—khi trong túi chẳng có đồng nào, và trên bờ cũng chẳng có gì đặc biệt để khiến tôi chú tâm, tôi nghĩ mình sẽ ra biển dạo chơi, xem xét phần thế giới là nước. Đó là cách tôi xua tan cơn phiền muộn và điều hòa sinh khí. Mỗi khi thấy miệng mím chặt, mỗi khi trong lòng u ám như một tháng mười một ẩm ướt, mưa phùn, tôi cho rằng đã đến lúc phải lên tàu đi biển càng sớm càng tốt.

scores.feedback: The translation is highly accurate and flows naturally in Vietnamese. While it captures the tone well, the archaic, slightly formal literary weight of Melville's original prose is slightly modernized, though this remains an excellent rendition.

results.translated_text: เป็นเรื่องจริงที่ไม่มีใครปฏิเสธได้ว่า ชายโสดผู้มั่งคั่งย่อมจำเป็นต้องมีคู่ครอง ไม่ว่าความปรารถนาหรือความคิดของชายผู้นั้นในยามแรกที่ย้ายเข้ามาอยู่ในละแวกนั้นจะเป็นอย่างไรก็ตาม ความจริงข้อนี้กลับฝังรากลึกในใจของครอบครัวโดยรอบจนทำให้เขาถูกมองว่าเป็นทรัพย์สมบัติอันชอบธรรมของลูกสาวคนใดคนหนึ่งของผู้หญิงในละแวกนั้น

scores.feedback: The translation is excellent and captures the ironic tone of the original well. The final phrase 'ของผู้หญิงในละแวกนั้น' is slightly redundant given 'ครอบครัวโดยรอบ' earlier, but overall it flows naturally and maintains the literary quality of Austen's prose.

results.translated_text: ในห้องสตูดิโอเต็มไปด้วยกลิ่นอันหอมเย้ายวนของดอกกุหลาบ และเมื่อสายลมฤดูเบาๆ รุกเร้าท่ามกลางต้นไม้ในสวน กลิ่นหอมหนักแน่นของดอกมะลิเทศหรือกลิ่นหอมละมุนละไมของดอกไม้หนามสีชมพูก็ลอยเข้ามาผ่านประตูที่เปิดอยู่ ที่มุมของโซฟาที่ทำจากถุงอานม้าเปอร์เซียซึ่งเขาเอนนอนอยู่บนนั้น กำลังสูบบุหรี่จำนวนนับไม่ถ้วนตามเคย ลอร์ดแฮรี วอตตันสามารถมองเห็นเพียงแวววาวของช่อดอกไม้สีทองอ่อนและกลิ่นหวานฉ่ำคล้ายน้ำผึ้งจากต้นลาเบอร์นัม

scores.feedback: The translation captures the imagery well, though 'lilac' is mistranslated as 'jasmine' (มะลิเทศ), and the phrasing 'สายลมฤดูเบาๆ' is slightly unnatural. While it conveys the aesthetic tone, some sentence structures feel a bit stiff compared to the fluid, decadent prose of the original.

results.translated_text:
"ท่านเคยอยู่ที่อัฟกานิสถาน ข้าพเจ้าสังเกตได้" 
"ท่านรู้ได้อย่างไรกันแน่?" ข้าพเจ้าถามด้วยความประหลาดใจ 
"ไม่ต้องกังวล," เขาพูดพร้อมหัวเราะให้ตัวเอง "คำถามในปัจจุบันคือเรื่องฮีโมโกลบิน ท่านไม่เห็นถึงความสำคัญของการค้นพบของข้าพเจ้าหรือไม่?" 
"เป็นเรื่องน่าสนใจในทางเคมีอย่างแน่นอน" ข้าพเจ้าตอบ "แต่ในทางปฏิบัติ..." 
"โอ้ ท่านชาย เรื่องนี้เป็นการค้นพบทางการแพทย์และนิติวิทยาศาสตร์ที่เป็นประโยชน์ที่สุดมาหลายปี ท่านไม่เห็นหรือว่ามันมอบวิธีการทดสอบคราบเลือดที่สมบูรณ์แบบที่สุดให้แก่เรา?"

scores.feedback: The translation is accurate in meaning, but the tone feels slightly stiff and modern rather than capturing the Victorian-era formality of Holmes and Watson. Phrases like 'ท่านชาย' for 'man' and 'ไม่ต้องกังวล' for 'Never mind' feel unnatural in this specific literary context.

results.translated_text: ข้าพเจ้าเข้าไปในป่าเพราะปรารถนาจะดำเนินชีวิตอย่างมีสติ เพื่อเผชิญหน้าเพียงแต่ข้อเท็จจริงอันจำเป็นของชีวิต และทดสอบว่าข้าพเจ้าสามารถเรียนรู้สิ่งที่มันต้องการสอนหรือไม่ มิใช่เมื่อถึงวาระแห่งความตายแล้วพบว่าข้าพเจ้าไม่ได้มีชีวิตอยู่ ข้าพเจ้าไม่ปรารถนาที่จะใช้ชีวิตที่ไม่ใช่ชีวิต เพราะชีวิตนั้นทรงคุณค่ายิ่งนัก มิใช่ข้าพเจ้าปรารถนาที่จะฝึกตนในทางยอมจำนน เว้นแต่จะเป็นสิ่งจำเป็นอย่างยิ่ง ข้าพเจ้าต้องการมีชีวิตอย่างลึกซึ้ง และดูดเอาน้ำหวานแห่งชีวิตออกมาจนหมดเกลี้ยง

scores.feedback: The translation is highly accurate and captures the philosophical tone well, though the metaphor 'suck out all the marrow' is slightly softened to 'suck out the nectar' (น้ำหวาน), which is a common cultural adaptation but loses the visceral intensity of the original.

results.translated_text: เรียกฉันว่า อิชเมเลก. เมื่อหลายปีก่อน—ไม่สนว่าจะนานสักเท่าใด—เมื่อฉันไม่มีเงินในกระเป๋าสักเท่าไหร่ และไม่มีอะไรน่าสนใจบนฝั่ง ฉันจึงคิดว่าคงจะออกเรือไปเที่ยวชมส่วนที่เป็นน้ำของโลกบ้าง วิธีนี้ของฉันช่วยขับไล่ความอึดอัดใจและปรับสมดุลการไหลเวียนของเลือด Whenever ฉันรู้สึกปากมุมคางขมุกขมัวWhenever จิตวิญญาณของฉันเต็มไปด้วยอากาศหนาวเหน็บและมีฝนตกพรำในเดือนพฤศจิกายน ฉันก็ถือว่าถึงเวลาต้องออกไปทะเลโดยด่วนที่สุด

scores.feedback: The translation suffers from significant errors, including the incorrect transliteration of 'Ishmael' and the failure to translate the English word 'Whenever,' which disrupts the flow entirely. While the initial meaning is conveyed, the lack of polish and the jarring inclusion of untranslated English words severely undermine the literary tone.

results.translated_text: यह एक सार्वत्रिक रूप से स्वीकृत सत्य है कि यदि एक अविवाहित पुरुष के पास अच्छी धनराशि है, तो उसकी विवाह हेतु इच्छा अनिवार्य रूप से होती है। हालाँकि वह व्यक्ति जब किसी नए क्षेत्र में प्रवेश करता है, तो उसकी भावनाएँ या दृष्टिकोण कितनी ही अज्ञात क्यों न हों, यह सत्य आस-पास के परिवारों की चेतना में इतनी गहराई से अंकित है कि उसे उनकी किसी एक बेटी का निश्चित रूप से विवाहार्थी समझा जाता है।

scores.feedback: The translation is highly accurate and captures the formal, ironic tone of Austen well. While the phrasing is slightly academic and less fluid than the original's biting wit, it remains a sophisticated and faithful rendering in Hindi.

results.translated_text: सुदीघर गुलाबों की गहरी सुगंध से भरा हुआ था, और जब बगीचे के पेड़ों में हल्की गर्मियों की हवा चलती थी, तो खुली खिड़की से बेल का भारी घना वास या गुलाबी पंखुड़ियों वाले शरक के नाजुक सुगंधित स्वाद का अनुभव होता था। वह फारसी सत्ता-बैगों पर लेटा हुआ था, जैसा कि उसका रूढ़िवादी अभ्यास था, निरंतर सिगरेटें पी रहा था; लॉर्ड हेनरी वोटन उस कोने से मधुमक्खियों के रंग के और शहद जैसे मिठे फूलों की चमक को आंखों में पकड़ सकता था, जो एक लाबर्नम के पेड़ पर खिले हुए थे।

scores.feedback: The translation suffers from significant errors, such as mistranslating 'studio' as 'sudighar' (a non-standard term), 'open door' as 'open window', and 'saddle-bags' as 'satta-bags' (nonsense). The phrasing is clunky and fails to capture Wilde's elegant, sensory-rich prose, making it read like a poor machine translation.

results.translated_text: "आप अफगानिस्तान गए हैं, मुझे ऐसा लगता है।" "आखिर आपने यह कैसे जान लिया?" मैं आश्चर्य में पूछा। "अब मत पूछें," वह हंसते हुए बोला। "अभी सवाल हीमोग्लोबिन के बारे में है। शायद आपको इस मेरी खोज के महत्व का अंदाजा हो?" "रसायन विज्ञान की दृष्टि से यह रोचक है, निस्संदेह," मैंने उत्तर दिया, "लेकिन व्यावहारिक रूप से—" "अरे मनुष्य, यह वर्षों की सबसे उपयोगी चिकित्सा-कानूनी खोज है। क्या आपको समझ नहीं आया कि यह खून के धब्बों की अविश्वसनीय परीक्षा प्रदान करती है?"

scores.feedback: The translation is generally accurate, but 'infallible' was mistranslated as 'unreliable' (अविश्वसनीय) instead of 'infallible/sure' (अचूक), and 'Why, man' was translated too literally as 'अरे मनुष्य', which sounds unnatural in Hindi.

results.translated_text: मैं जंगल में इसलिए गया क्योंकि मैं जानबूझकर जीना चाहता था, केवल जीवन की आवश्यक तथ्यों का सामना करना चाहता था, और यह देखना चाहता था कि क्या मैं उसकी उपलब्धियों से कुछ सीख सकता हूं, और मरते समय यह न पाऊं कि मैंने नहीं जीया। मैंने जीवन को वह रूप देना नहीं चाहा जो जीवन नहीं है; क्योंकि जीवन इतना प्यारा है। ना ही मैंने त्याग का अनुसरण करना चाहता था, जब तक कि उसकी वास्तविक आवश्यकता न हो जाए। मैं गहराई से जीना चाहता था और जीवन की हड्डियों को चूस लेना चाहता था।

scores.feedback: The translation captures the core meaning well, but suffers from awkward phrasing (e.g., 'जीवन की हड्डियों को चूस लेना' is too literal and loses the poetic impact of 'marrow') and some grammatical inconsistencies in the Hindi sentence structure.

results.translated_text: मुझे इसमेल कहकर बुलाएं। कुछ वर्ष पहले—कितने बिल्कुल, यह मायने नहीं रखता—जब मेरे पास जेब में कम या नगण्य धन था और सुदूर स्थल पर मुझे किसी विशेष बात का आकर्षण भी नहीं था, मैंने सोचा कि थोड़ा साफ़राह करूँ और संसार के जल भाग को देख लूँ। यह मेरी एक आदत है जिससे मूख्यता मिटती है और रक्त-संचार सही होता है। जब भी मुझे ऐसा लगता है कि मुंह पर कड़वाहट आ रही है; या जब भी मेरे मन में घना, ठंडी बारिश वाला नवंबर हो जाता है, मैं तुरंत समुद्र में जाने के लिए अपना समय बहुत उचित मान लेता हूँ।

scores.feedback: The translation captures the literal meaning well, but struggles with the archaic, rhythmic tone of Melville; phrases like 'मूख्यता मिटती है' for 'driving off the spleen' miss the idiomatic nuance, and the sentence structure feels slightly stiff in Hindi.

scores.feedback: The translation is highly readable and captures the tone well, though 'неуловимый' (elusive) is an incorrect translation for 'infallible' (безошибочный/верный), which is a significant semantic error in this context.

results.translated_text: Я ушёл в лес, чтобы жить по-настоящему, столкнуться лицом к лицу только с основными фактами жизни и понять, чему она может научить, а не обнаружить при смерти, что я не жил вовсе. Я не желал жить тем, что жизнью не является, ведь жизнь так дорога; я также не желал практиковать смирение, если оно не было неизбежным. Я хотел прожить жизнь глубоко и высосать из неё всю суть.

scores.feedback: The translation is highly readable and captures the core meaning well, though it slightly softens the philosophical weight of 'deliberately' (translated as 'по-настоящему' rather than 'осознанно') and loses the vivid metaphor of 'suck out all the marrow' by substituting it with the more generic 'высосать всю суть'.

results.translated_text:

Некоторое время назад — неважно сколько именно, — имея немного или вовсе не имея денег в кошельке и ничего такого, что интересовало бы меня на берегу, я решил отправиться морем, осмотреть водную часть мира. Это мой способ прогнать хандру и нормализовать кровообращение. Как только я замечаю, что мои губы сжимаются в грустную гримасу или когда в моей душе наступает сырая, дождливая ноябрьская тьма, я считаю необходимым как можно скорее отправиться в море.

scores.feedback: The translation is accurate and readable, but it misses the archaic, rhythmic gravitas of Melville's prose, particularly in the famous opening line which is traditionally translated as 'Зовите меня Измаил' rather than the literal 'Позовите меня Исмаил'.

results.translated_text: Це істина, загальновизнана, що одинакий чоловік у володінні доброю статком, повинен потребувати дружини. results.translated_text: "Ви були в Афганістані, я розумію." — "Як же ви це дізналися?" — здивовано запитав я. — "Не важливо", — сказав він, сміючись під ніс. — "Тепер справа в гемоглобіні. results.translated_text: Я пішов у ліс, бо прагнув жити свідомо, стикнутися лише з найсуттєвішими фактами життя і спробувати вивчити його уроки, а коли прийде час померти, не виявити, що я не жив. Декілька років тому — не важливо, скільки саме, — не маючи ні грошей у гаманці, ані жодної особливої справи на березі, я вирішив потримати курс по хвилях і оглянути водяну частину світу. The tone is appropriately literary, though it misses some of the specific archaic weight of Melville's 19th-century prose.

scores.feedback: The translation contains significant errors, such as translating 'sail' as 'on wheels' (על גלגלים) and 'interest me' as 'excite me' (לרגוש), which fundamentally distort Melville's meaning.
